title: fagus hayatae subsp. pashanica
intro: Fagus hayatae subsp. pashanica, also known as Pashan Tea Tree, is a subspecies of the Fagus hayatae tree, native to China and primarily found in the mountainous regions of the country. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, making it useful in traditional medicine for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Fagus hayatae subsp. pashanica include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and protect against infections. In herbal preparations, the leaves and twigs of the plant are often dried and used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
